operating_state: Total revenue increased $3.3 billion to $101.9 billion driven by higher noninterest income of $4.2 billion, partially offset by lower net interest income of $871 million. Investment banking fees increased from higher debt and equity issuance activity while investment and brokerage services rose on elevated market valuations and positive AUM flows. Net interest yield decreased 13 basis points to 1.95 percent primarily from higher deposit costs despite higher asset yields. Commercial loan portfolio expanded $37.1 billion during 2024 while consumer loan portfolio increased driven by credit card and securities-based lending growth.
cost_structure: Noninterest expense increased $967 million to $66.8 billion driven by revenue-related expenses and investments in people, operations and technology, partially offset by lower FDIC expenses. The efficiency ratio improved to 65.57 percent from 66.79 percent. Provision for credit losses increased $1.4 billion to $5.8 billion primarily from credit card and small business loan growth plus asset quality deterioration in commercial real estate office and credit card portfolios. Net charge-offs increased to $4.2 billion with the net charge-off ratio rising to 0.93% from 0.68%.
material_events: The Board declared a quarterly common dividend of $0.26 per share and authorized a $25 billion common stock repurchase program effective August 1, 2024. The Corporation repurchased $13.1 billion of common stock during 2024. Total long-term debt decreased $18.9 billion to $283.3 billion during 2024 due to maturities and redemptions exceeding new issuances. G-SIB surcharge increased 50 basis points effective January 1, 2024, raising the long-term debt regulatory minimum requirement to 9.0% from 8.5%.
macro_exposures: The Corporation faces exposure to interest rate adjustments, inflation, currency exchange rates, economic conditions, trade policies including increased tariffs, and geopolitical instability. Commercial real estate office portfolio faces continued performance pressure with significant criticized exposure and near-term maturities. Uninsured deposits totaled $771.1 billion at December 31, 2024. Climate risk management framework addresses both physical risks from extreme weather events and transition risks from low-carbon economy shifts.
forward_outlook: Management continues investments in people, technology and operations to support growth initiatives while maintaining capital planning processes aligned with regulatory requirements including annual CCAR submissions and stress testing. The Corporation maintains senior notes becoming TLAC ineligible during 2025-2029. Interest-only residential mortgages will not require fully-amortizing payments until 2026 or later.
uncertainty_profile: Future credit losses may exceed current expectations due to changes in economic assumptions, customer behavior and adverse economic developments. Pending litigation and regulatory investigations across multiple areas including unemployment benefits processing, credit card payment services, anti-money laundering programs and electronic payments processing remain in various stages. Cybersecurity threats increased in frequency and severity due to evolving threat environment including AI-enabled attack methodologies. Credit rating downgrades could trigger additional collateral requirements under OTC derivative contracts and impact access to short-term funding sources.
